issue_title: Add a visual moments editor for captions and overlays
issue_body:
Build the next creator-facing editing stage after setup, style, canvas, and audio polish: a full-episode moments workspace where the creator can review a transcript-style timeline and add polished visual treatments at key points. It should let them create and edit caption sections, title moments, b-roll placeholders, branded callouts, and simple overlay notes without manually editing every frame. This is the next highest-value step because the product can already collect episode inputs, choose a look, customize layout, and polish audio, but it still lacks the contextual editing layer that makes a long-form episode feel deliberately produced.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A reviewer can open the app, move from an episode into a new visual moments/editing view, see a full-episode timeline or transcript-style list with speaker-aware moments, add at least four moment types: captions, title moment, b-roll overlay, and visual callout, edit their timing/text/visibility, preview how the selected moment affects the episode look, and see those edits persist when navigating away and back. The UI must stay creator-facing with clear quality choices and no internal pipeline language. The existing verification command `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` must pass, with focused test coverage for creating, editing, persisting, and previewing visual moments.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The completed work covers setup, preset styling, reusable canvas customization, and audio polish. The largest missing product capability before export is the contextual visual editing that turns a raw long-form recording into a polished episode: captions, b-roll, title moments, and callouts placed at meaningful points across the conversation._
